_meta.description: Per-code preferences for fitness scoring. Bias preference and threshold are literature-anchored conservative estimates; cycle_time_us is typical surface-code syndrome extraction round, overridden where families differ. See notes per entry for refs.

surface.notes: Standard unrotated surface code, depolarizing threshold ~1%. Fowler et al.

rotated_surface.notes: Rotated layout, same threshold as standard. Google Willow uses this.

toric.notes: Surface code on torus. Kitaev 1997. Periodic BC = no boundary.

surface-17.notes: Distance-3 rotated surface, 17 physical qubits. Tomita & Svore 2014.

xysurface.notes: XY surface code, higher threshold under depolarizing. Tuckett et al. 2020.

clifford-deformed_surface.notes: Clifford-deformed variants tunable. Dua et al. 2024.

triangle_surface.notes: Triangular boundary surface code, slightly lower threshold.

xzzx.notes: XZZX surface, threshold rises with bias η. ~3% at η~10, up to ~50% asymptotically. Bonilla Ataides et al. 2021.

xzzx_7_1_3.notes: Small XZZX [[7,1,3]] variant. Bias-tailored small code.

xzzx_10_2_3.notes: Small XZZX [[10,2,3]] variant. Bias-tailored small code.

color.notes: Generic 2D color code. Threshold ~0.3%, transversal Cliffords. Bombin & Martin-Delgado 2006.

2d_color.notes: Same as above, more specific tag.

488_color.notes: Square-octagon (4.8.8) color code, slightly higher threshold than 6.6.6.

bacon_shor.notes: Subsystem code, no asymptotic threshold but ~0.2% pseudo-threshold. Aliferis & Cross 2007.

bacon_shor_9.notes: d=9 Bacon-Shor instance, demonstrated on Honeywell ion trap (Chen et al.).

heavy_hex.notes: Compass code variant, IBM heavy-hex lattice. Threshold ~0.45%. Chamberland et al. 2020.

quantum_repetition.notes: Single-axis repetition code, very high threshold but only one error type. Needs extreme bias to be useful.

shor_nine.notes: Concatenated [[9,1,3]] Shor code. Low pseudo-threshold; mainly a baseline.

steane.notes: [[7,1,3]] self-dual CSS, distance 3. Used as concatenation target. Pseudo-threshold ~10^-4.
